[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Musician%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Musician%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Musician%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Musician%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(May 2022)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

is someone who [composes](/wiki/Composer "Composer"), [conducts](/wiki/Conducting "Conducting"), or [performs](/wiki/Performing_arts#Performers "Performing arts") [music](/wiki/Music "Music").[[1]](#cite_note-1) According to the [United States Employment Service](/wiki/United_States_Employment_Service "United States Employment Service"), "musician" is a general [term](/wiki/Terminology "Terminology") used to designate a person who follows music as a [profession](/wiki/Profession "Profession").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Musicians include [songwriters](/wiki/Songwriter "Songwriter"), who write both [music](/wiki/Music "Music") and [lyrics](/wiki/Lyrics "Lyrics") for [songs](/wiki/Songs "Songs"); conductors, who direct a musical performance; and performers, who perform for an audience. A music performer is generally either a [singer](/wiki/Singer "Singer") (also known as a vocalist), who provides vocals, or an instrumentalist, who plays a [musical instrument](/wiki/Musical_instrument "Musical instrument"). Musicians may perform on their own or as part of a [group, band or orchestra](/wiki/Musical_ensemble "Musical ensemble").

[*Dictionary of Occupational Titles, Volume 1*](https://books.google.com/books?id=BsOj3BYD4DUC&pg=PA883). [U.S. Government Printing Office](/wiki/U.S._Government_Printing_Office "U.S. Government Printing Office"). 1949. p. 883.

["Recording Artist (or Group)"](https://web.archive.org/web/20201028095241/https://www.berklee.edu/careers/roles/recording-group-or-band). [Berklee College of Music](/wiki/Berklee_College_of_Music "Berklee College of Music"). January 2011. Archived from [the original](https://www.berklee.edu/careers/roles/recording-group-or-band) on 28 October 2020. Retrieved 19 February 2021.

[Sir George Grove](/wiki/Sir_George_Grove "Sir George Grove"), [John Alexander Fuller Maitland](/wiki/John_Alexander_Fuller_Maitland "John Alexander Fuller Maitland"), eds. (1922). *[A Dictionary of Music and Musicians, Volume 1](https://books.google.com/books?id=YgBKAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA588#v=twopage&q=conducting%20dictionary)*, p. 581. Macmillan.

For other uses, see [Musician (disambiguation)](/wiki/Musician_(disambiguation) "Musician (disambiguation)").

Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Musician"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Musician%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Musician%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Musician%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Musician%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Musician%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Musician%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(May 2022)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Person who composes, conducts or performs music

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b5/Ed_Sheeran_in_Philadelphia_01.jpg/250px-Ed_Sheeran_in_Philadelphia_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ed_Sheeran_in_Philadelphia_01.jpg)

British singer-songwriter [Ed Sheeran](/wiki/Ed_Sheeran "Ed Sheeran") in 2023.

A **musician** is someone who [composes](/wiki/Composer "Composer"), [conducts](/wiki/Conducting "Conducting"), or [performs](/wiki/Performing_arts#Performers "Performing arts") [music](/wiki/Music "Music").[[1]](#cite_note-1) According to the [United States Employment Service](/wiki/United_States_Employment_Service "United States Employment Service"), "musician" is a general [term](/wiki/Terminology "Terminology") used to designate a person who follows music as a [profession](/wiki/Profession "Profession").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Musicians include [songwriters](/wiki/Songwriter "Songwriter"), who write both [music](/wiki/Music "Music") and [lyrics](/wiki/Lyrics "Lyrics") for [songs](/wiki/Songs "Songs"); conductors, who direct a musical performance; and performers, who perform for an audience. A music performer is generally either a [singer](/wiki/Singer "Singer") (also known as a vocalist), who provides vocals, or an instrumentalist, who plays a [musical instrument](/wiki/Musical_instrument "Musical instrument"). Musicians may perform on their own or as part of a [group, band or orchestra](/wiki/Musical_ensemble "Musical ensemble").

Musicians may specialize in a [musical genre](/wiki/Musical_genre "Musical genre"), although many perform across a variety of styles and often blend or cross genres. Their musical output is influenced by a range of factors, including culture, skill set, life experience, education, and creative preferences. A musician who [records](/wiki/Sound_recording_and_reproduction "Sound recording and reproduction") and [releases music](/wiki/Music_release "Music release") is often referred to as a **recording artist**;[[3]](#cite_note-3) while a musician who primarily writes and records [art music](/wiki/Art_music "Art music"), media [soundtracks](/wiki/Soundtrack "Soundtrack") , and scores—including for [films](/wiki/Film_score "Film score"), [theatrical stage productions](/w/index.php?title=Theatre_score&action=edit&redlink=1 "Theatre score (page does not exist)"), and [video games](/wiki/Video_game_music "Video game music")—or [classical music](/wiki/Classical_music "Classical music") is usually called a [composer](/wiki/Composer "Composer"). A [music producer](/wiki/Music_producer "Music producer"), while not a musician outright, may themselves be a musician. A [conductor](/wiki/Conducting "Conducting"), by way of gestures, leads and provides live direction for, or *conducts*, an ensemble, typically an orchestra or choral group.

## Types

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Musician&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Types")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/George_Michael_at_Antwerp_%28BRAVO%29.jpg/250px-George_Michael_at_Antwerp_%28BRAVO%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:George_Michael_at_Antwerp_(BRAVO).jpg)

[George Michael](/wiki/George_Michael "George Michael") at Antwerp.

### Composer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Musician&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Composer")]

Main articles: [Composer](/wiki/Composer "Composer") and [Songwriter](/wiki/Songwriter "Songwriter")

A composer is a musician who creates [musical compositions](/wiki/Musical_compositions "Musical compositions"). The title is principally used for those who write [classical music](/wiki/Classical_music "Classical music") or [film music](/wiki/Film_music "Film music"). Those who write the music for [popular songs](/wiki/Popular_songs "Popular songs") may be called [songwriters](/wiki/Songwriters "Songwriters"). Those who mainly write the words for songs may be referred to as [lyricists](/wiki/Lyricist "Lyricist").

### Conductor

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Musician&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Conductor")]

Main article: [Conducting](/wiki/Conducting "Conducting")

A conductor directs a musical performance; conducting has been defined as "the art of directing the simultaneous performance of several players or singers by the use of gesture".[[4]](#cite_note-4) The conductor stands on a raised podium and communicates with the musicians through hand gestures or eye contact.

### Performer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Musician&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Performer")]

Main article: [Performing arts § Music](/wiki/Performing_arts#Music "Performing arts")

Examples of performers include, but are not limited to, instrumentalists and singers who perform for an audience. A musician can perform as a [solo artist](/wiki/Solo_music "Solo music") or as a part of an ensemble (e.g. an [orchestra](/wiki/Orchestra "Orchestra"), a [choir](/wiki/Choir "Choir") or a pop group).

### Producer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Musician&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Producer")]

Main article: [Record producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer")

A music producer plays a crucial role in shaping the sound of an album or song, overseeing the recording process, and guiding the overall creative direction. They often collaborate closely with artists, musicians, sound engineers, and other industry professionals.
